message.content:
@startuml
title Fabric Private Chaincode - Enclave registry - Current approach

actor Admin as Admin
participant ECC as ECC
participant ERCC as ERCC
participant IAS as IAS
participant Orderer as Orderer
participant Peers as Peers

subgraph instantiation
    direction TB
    Admin -> ECC : install
    ECC -> ECC : instantiate(expected_mrenclave)
    ECC -> Admin : success / error
endsubgraph

subgraph register new enclave
    direction TB
    Admin -> ECC : setup
    ECC -> ERCC : create
    ERCC -> ECC : quote, ECC_s_pk
    ERCC -> ECC : register(quote, ECC_s_pk)
    ECC -> Orderer : send quote
    Orderer -> ERCC : Attestation
    ERCC -> Orderer : putState(erccc.ECC_s_pk, Attestation)
    ERCC -> ECC : success / error
endsubgraph

subgraph register validation
    direction TB
    Admin -> Orderer : submit setup transaction
    Orderer -> Peers : broadcast block
    Peers -> Peers : Validation
    Peers -> ERCC : Normal validation
    Peers -> ERCC : Custom ERCC validation
    ERCC -> ERCC_VSCC : expected_mrenclave = getState(ecc.MRENCLAVE)
    ERCC_VSCC -> ERCC : check attestation signature
    ERCC_VSCC -> ERCC : check attestation ECC_s_pk and mrenclave
endsubgraph

subgraph Drawbacks
    direction TB
    note right of Orderer
        Drawbacks
        - Single-endorser policy only + Custom validation at every peer
        - ERCC must contact IAS (Internet connection and IAS credentials needed)
        - Current impl: No way to securely pass credentials to ERCC only
        - for EPID attestation SPID is needed when quote is created (ECC must provide this)
    end note
endsubgraph

@enduml
